metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2892
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Supplemental Fig. S3: "FILIP regulates the subcellular distribution of NMHC IIb
and modulates NMHC IIb binding to F-actins"

Supplemental Fig. S6: "Knockdown of NMHC IIb results in the elongation of spine
length"
- "Whereas the stress fibre-like distribution of myosin 2b was dominant in COS-7 cells without endogenous FILIP, the number of cells with granular myosin 2b distribution surpassed that of cells with stress fibre-like distribution in the presence of FILIP (see Supplemental Fig. S3a, b online). We next investigated whether FILIP influenced the formation of actin stress fibres, as myosin 2 is a component of actin stress fibres21,22. The number of cells with actin stress fibres decreased in the presence of FILIP (see Supplemental Fig. S3c, d online). Because FILIP binds to the N-terminal domain of myosin 2b, which is essential for binding to actin fibres, and the binding of myosin 2b to actin fibres is essential for the function of myosin 2b, we hypothesised that FILIP interferes with the activity of myosin 2b. We then compared the appearance of NMHC IIb in COS-7 cells in the presence of the myosin 2b inhibitor blebbistatin. Blebbistatin binds to the myosin globular head domain and blocks the rigid binding of myosin 2b to actin23. ". Experimental description: - "The mice were maintained in the animal room at the Division of Laboratory Animal Resources,..."
